seoTitle: BYD Semiconductor Products Distributor | IGBT, SiC MOSFET, IPM | BeiLuo
seoDescription: Browse BYD Semiconductor product categories: IGBT Modules, SiC MOSFETs, IPM, and Power MOSFETs. In stock with technical support.

faqs.question: How do I select the right BYD power module for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right BYD power module involves several key steps: (1) Determine your voltage requirements - 650V/750V for 400V systems, 1200V for 800V systems. (2) Calculate current requirements based on power rating and thermal constraints. (3) Choose between IGBT and SiC based on switching frequency and efficiency needs. (4) Consider package type for thermal and mechanical compatibility. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance and thermal calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application requirements for personalized module selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BYD IGBT and SiC MOSFET modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT and SiC MOSFET modules have distinct characteristics: IGBTs are cost-effective for 10-20kHz applications with good efficiency, available in 650V-1200V ratings. SiC MOSFETs offer 90% lower switching losses, support 50-100kHz operation, provide 1-3% higher efficiency, and enable 3-5x power density improvement. SiC is ideal for high-frequency, high-efficiency applications like EV charging, while IGBT is preferred for cost-sensitive motor drive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SiC for high-frequency high-efficiency applications, IGBT for cost-sensitive standard frequency applications.

faqs.question: Are BYD power modules available for immediate delivery?
faqs.answer: Yes, most BYD power modules are in stock with standard lead times of 4-6 weeks. Popular models like BG300F08A13L5, BG150G12F13L4, and BM840F12B34U2 are regularly stocked. For large volume orders or specific delivery requirements, please contact our sales team. We also offer sample quantities for evaluation purposes with expedited delivery options available for urgent projects.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales for current stock status and delivery schedules for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What technical support does BYD provide for power module applications?
faqs.answer: BYD provides comprehensive technical support including: (1) Application notes and design guides for gate drive, thermal management, and PCB layout. (2) Simulation models (PLECS, PSPICE) for circuit simulation. (3) FAE support for design reviews and troubleshooting. (4) Reference designs for EV motor drives, charging, and solar applications. (5) Thermal calculation and heatsink selection assistance. Contact our FAE team for any technical questions during your design process.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design phase for optimal support and guidance.

faqs.question: Do BYD power modules meet automotive quality standards?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD power modules are AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications. The modules undergo rigorous reliability testing including high temperature reverse bias (HTRB), temperature cycling, high humidity high temperature reverse bias (H3TRB), and intermittent operating life (IOL) tests. These modules are used in BYD's own vehicles, providing proven reliability in real-world automotive environments. Qualification reports are available upon request.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request automotive qualification reports from our sales team for your quality documentation.

categories.name: IGBT Modules
categories.description: BYD IGBT power modules feature trench field-stop technology, offering low switching losses and high reliability for industrial and automotive applications.
categories.longDescription: BYD IGBT power modules adopt advanced trench field-stop technology, providing low switching losses, high power density, and excellent thermal cycling capability. The product range covers 650V-1200V voltage platforms with various current ratings from 75A to 450A. These modules are widely used in new energy vehicles, industrial motor drives, solar inverters, and UPS systems. All products are AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications. Contact our distributor team for BYD IGBT module selection and technical support.

series.name: 650V IGBT Modules
series.description: Optimized for 400V EV systems and industrial drives

series.name: 1200V IGBT Modules
series.description: High voltage modules for 800V EV platforms and industrial applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD IGBT Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right IGBT module based on voltage, current, switching frequency, and thermal requirements

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for BYD IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT modules are available in voltage ratings from 650V to 1200V: (1) 650V series for 400V EV systems and industrial motor drives. (2) 750V series for commercial vehicle applications. (3) 1200V series for 800V EV platforms and high-power industrial applications. Select voltage rating with adequate margin above DC bus voltage. Contact our distributor team for detailed voltage selection guidance based on your specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the difference between F-type and G-type IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT modules use different suffixes to indicate package and configuration: (1) F-type: Standard package with fast switching characteristics. (2) G-type: Low switching loss optimized for high-frequency applications. (3) I-type: Three-level NPC topology for solar inverters. (4) B-type: Half-bridge configuration. (5) H-type: High current density design. Select based on topology and switching frequency requirements. Our distributor team can help you select the right module type.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for module type selection based on your circuit topology.

faqs.question: What is the typical VCE(sat) of BYD IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT modules feature low VCE(sat) characteristics: (1) 650V series: 1.45-1.55V typical at rated current. (2) 1200V series: 1.7-1.85V typical at rated current. The low saturation voltage minimizes conduction losses, improving system efficiency. Temperature coefficient is positive, ensuring good current sharing in parallel operation. Contact our distributor for detailed loss calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for loss calculations and efficiency analysis.

faqs.question: Are BYD IGBT modules automotive qualified?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD IGBT modules are AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications. The modules undergo extensive reliability testing including: (1) High temperature reverse bias (HTRB). (2) Temperature cycling. (3) High humidity high temperature reverse bias (H3TRB). (4) Intermittent operating life (IOL). These modules are used in BYD's own vehicles, proving their reliability in real-world automotive environments. Contact our distributor for qualification reports.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive qualification reports and reliability data.

faqs.question: What packages are available for BYD IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BYD IGBT modules are available in industry-standard packages: (1) 34mm package: Compact design for low to medium power applications. (2) 62mm package: Industry standard for medium to high power applications. (3) EasyPACK: Press-fit pins for easy assembly. (4) Custom packages: Available for high-volume applications. All packages use advanced materials for optimal thermal performance. Contact our distributor for package selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package selection based on your thermal and mechanical requirements.

products.shortDescription: 750V 300A IGBT module for EV motor drives. Features trench field-stop technology with low switching losses.
products.description: BG300F08A13L5 is a 750V 300A IGBT power module featuring advanced trench field-stop technology. It offers low switching losses, high power density, and excellent thermal cycling capability. The module is ideal for new energy vehicle motor drives, industrial inverters, and solar applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 750V 300A IGBT power module with trench field-stop technology. Optimized for 400V EV systems and industrial drives.
Low VCE(sat) of 1.55V typical at 25°C. Fast switching with low Eon and Eoff losses.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support for gate drive and thermal design.

faqs.question: What is the VCE(sat) of BG300F08A13L5?
faqs.answer: BG300F08A13L5 typical VCE(sat) is 1.55V at 25°C with IC=300A. At 150°C junction temperature, VCE(sat) increases to approximately 1.85V. The low saturation voltage minimizes conduction losses, contributing to high system efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for applications requiring low conduction losses and high efficiency.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency range?
faqs.answer: BG300F08A13L5 is optimized for 10-20kHz switching frequency. It can operate up to 25kHz with proper thermal management. For higher switching frequencies, consider BYD SiC MOSFET modules. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 10-20kHz for optimal efficiency and thermal performance.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is required?
faqs.answer: BG300F08A13L5 requires: +15V turn-on for low VCE(sat), -8V turn-off for fast switching and dv/dt immunity. Gate resistor: 5-15Ω depending on switching speed requirements. Use isolated IGBT gate drivers with adequate drive current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use isolated gate drivers with +15V/-8V output and 4A+ peak current.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.18K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. The low thermal resistance enables high power density designs. Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation and maximum ambient temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate heatsink thermal resistance based on power dissipation and ambient temperature.

faqs.question: Can BG300F08A13L5 be used for EV motor drives?
faqs.answer: Yes, BG300F08A13L5 is specifically designed for EV motor drives. The 750V rating is ideal for 400V EV platforms, and the 300A rating supports 75-150kW motor drives. The module is AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for 400V EV motor drives up to 150kW.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BG300F08A13L5 and BG400F08A13L5?
faqs.answer: BG300F08A13L5 and BG400F08A13L5 have the same voltage rating (750V) and package (62mm) but different current ratings (300A vs 400A). BG400 uses larger die and has slightly higher VCE(sat). Select based on current requirements and thermal constraints.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BG300 for 75-150kW applications. Select BG400 for 150-200kW applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - EV Applications
faeReview.content: BG300F08A13L5 is one of our most popular IGBT modules for EV motor drives. The 750V/300A rating is perfect for 400V EV platforms, and the low losses contribute to high system efficiency. We've successfully used this module in numerous EV projects with excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: Popular choice for 400V EV motor drives

alternativeParts.comparison: BG300F08A13L5=><BG400F08A13L5: Output current 400A > 300A (+33%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current for larger motor drives

alternativeParts.comparison: BG300F08A13L5=><BG150G12F13L4: Output current 150A < 300A (lower), suitable for direct replacement

Low VCE(sat) of 1.75V typical at 25°C. Fast switching with low losses.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support.

faqs.question: What applications is BG150G12F13L4 designed for?
faqs.answer: BG150G12F13L4 is designed for 800V EV platforms and high-voltage industrial applications. The 1200V rating provides adequate margin for 800V DC bus voltage. Typical applications include 800V EV motor drives (100-200kW), high-voltage industrial inverters, and solar inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 800V EV systems and high-voltage industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of BG150G12F13L4?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.25K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. The module can handle significant power dissipation with proper heatsinking. For high-power applications, consider liquid cooling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for continuous high-power operation.

faqs.question: Can BG150G12F13L5 be paralleled for higher current?
faqs.answer: Yes, BG150G12F13L4 can be paralleled for higher current capability. The positive temperature coefficient of VCE(sat) ensures good current sharing. Use matched devices and symmetrical layout for best results.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use matched devices with symmetrical layout for parallel operation.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - High Voltage Applications
faeReview.content: BG150G12F13L4 is our go-to module for 800V EV applications. The 1200V rating provides excellent margin, and the switching characteristics are well-optimized. We've seen great success in 800V EV motor drive applications.

alternativeParts.comparison: BG150G12F13L4=><BM600F12B34U2: Output current 400A > 150A (+167%), suitable for direct replacement
alternativeParts.reason: Higher current and efficiency with SiC technology

products.shortDescription: 650V 200A I-Type three-level NPC IGBT module for solar inverters. Features low switching losses.
products.description: BG200I07N10H6 is a 650V 200A I-Type three-level NPC IGBT power module. It is optimized for solar inverters and high-efficiency motor drives requiring three-level topology.
descriptionParagraphs: 650V 200A I-Type three-level NPC IGBT module. Optimized for T-type and NPC topologies.
Low switching losses enable high-frequency operation. Ideal for solar inverters.
Industrial grade reliability. In stock with FAE support.

faqs.question: What is the I-Type package?
faqs.answer: The I-Type package is specifically designed for three-level NPC (Neutral Point Clamped) and T-type topologies. It contains the necessary IGBT and diode configuration for three-level converters, enabling higher efficiency and lower EMI compared to two-level topologies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for three-level NPC or T-type converter designs.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of three-level topology?
faqs.answer: Three-level topology advantages: (1) Lower switching losses - approximately 50% reduction. (2) Lower EMI due to reduced voltage step. (3) Better output waveform quality. (4) Higher efficiency - typically 0.5-1% improvement. (5) Suitable for high-power solar inverters and motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use three-level topology for high-efficiency applications >10kW.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Solar Applications
faeReview.content: BG200I07N10H6 is excellent for solar inverter applications. The three-level topology provides significant efficiency improvements, and the I-Type package simplifies design. We've seen great results in 10-50kW solar inverters.

alternativeParts.comparison: BG200I07N10H6=><BG300F08A13L5: Output current 300A > 200A (higher), suitable for direct replacement

products.name: IGBT Modules BG200I07N10H6

products.shortDescription: 750V 400A high-current IGBT module for EV motor drives. Features low VCE(sat) and high reliability.
products.description: BG400F08A13L5 is a 750V 400A high-current IGBT power module. It provides high current capability for large EV motor drives, commercial vehicles, and high-power industrial applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 750V 400A high-current IGBT power module. Designed for high-power applications.
Low VCE(sat) of 1.65V typical at 25°C. High current density design.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support.

faqs.question: What applications require BG400F08A13L5?
faqs.answer: BG400F08A13L5 is designed for high-power applications requiring 300A+ continuous current. Typical applications include: (1) 150-200kW EV motor drives. (2) Commercial vehicle powertrains. (3) High-power industrial motor drives (>100kW). (4) Large UPS systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-power applications requiring >300A continuous current.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required?
faqs.answer: BG400F08N13L5 requires robust thermal management due to high power dissipation. Recommended: (1) Heatsink thermal resistance <0.15K/W. (2) Liquid cooling preferred for continuous operation. (3) High-performance thermal interface material. (4) Thermal simulation recommended for worst-case analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use liquid cooling for continuous high-power operation.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - High Power Applications
faeReview.content: BG400F08A13L5 is our high-current workhorse for demanding applications. The 400A rating and robust design make it ideal for commercial vehicles and high-power industrial drives. Proper thermal design is critical for this module.

alternativeParts.comparison: BG400F08A13L5=><BG300F08A13L5: Output current 300A < 400A (-25%), suitable for direct replacement

categories.name: SiC MOSFET Modules
categories.description: BYD SiC MOSFET power modules feature advanced silicon carbide technology, offering ultra-low switching losses and high-frequency operation for next-generation power systems.
categories.longDescription: BYD SiC MOSFET power modules utilize advanced silicon carbide technology to deliver breakthrough performance in power conversion applications. With switching losses reduced by 90% compared to silicon IGBTs, these modules enable higher switching frequencies (50-100kHz), smaller passive components, and higher system efficiency. The product range includes 1200V modules with current ratings from 300A to 550A, suitable for EV fast charging, solar inverters, and high-performance motor drives. Contact our distributor team for BYD SiC MOSFET module selection and technical support.

series.name: 1200V SiC MOSFET Modules
series.description: High-voltage SiC modules for 800V EV charging and high-efficiency inverters

series.name: High-Current SiC Modules
series.description: Ultra-high current SiC modules for megawatt-level applications

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD SiC MOSFET Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right SiC module based on voltage, current, switching frequency, and efficiency requirements

faqs.question: What are the advantages of BYD SiC MOSFET modules over IGBTs?
faqs.answer: BYD SiC MOSFET modules offer significant advantages: (1) Switching losses reduced by 90% compared to IGBTs. (2) Support 50-100kHz switching frequency vs 10-20kHz for IGBTs. (3) System efficiency improvement of 1-3%. (4) Power density increase of 3-5x. (5) Higher temperature operation up to 175°C. (6) No tail current during turn-off. These advantages make SiC ideal for EV charging and high-efficiency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for ROI analysis comparing SiC and IGBT solutions.

faqs.question: What gate drive requirements do SiC MOSFETs have?
faqs.answer: BYD SiC MOSFETs require specific gate drive: (1) +18V turn-on voltage for lowest RDS(on). (2) -4V turn-off voltage to prevent dv/dt induced turn-on. (3) Gate resistance 2-8Ω depending on switching speed. (4) High CMTI (>100V/ns) gate drivers. (5) Kelvin source connection recommended. Use dedicated SiC gate drivers, not standard IGBT drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for gate drive circuit design recommendations.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for BYD SiC modules?
faqs.answer: BYD SiC MOSFET modules are available in 1200V rating. The 1200V rating is suitable for 800V EV charging systems and high-voltage industrial applications. Higher voltage ratings (1700V) are under development. Contact our distributor for the latest product roadmap.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1200V SiC modules for 800V EV charging and high-voltage applications.

faqs.question: Are BYD SiC modules automotive qualified?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD SiC MOSFET modules are AEC-Q101 qualified for automotive applications. The modules undergo rigorous reliability testing including high temperature operation, temperature cycling, and humidity testing. These modules are suitable for EV onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and traction inverters.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for automotive qualification reports.

faqs.question: What packages are available for BYD SiC modules?
faqs.answer: BYD SiC MOSFET modules use industry-standard 34mm packages. The 34mm package provides excellent thermal performance and is compatible with standard heatsinks and mounting hardware. The compact size enables high power density designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package drawings and thermal models.

products.shortDescription: 1200V 500A SiC MOSFET module for EV charging. Features ultra-low switching losses and high-frequency operation.
products.description: BM840F12B34U2 is a 1200V 500A SiC MOSFET power module featuring advanced silicon carbide technology. It provides ultra-low switching losses and supports high-frequency operation for next-generation power systems.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 500A SiC MOSFET module with advanced trench SiC technology. Ultra-low switching losses enable high-frequency operation.
Typical RDS(on) of 2.5mΩ at 25°C. Switching losses 90% lower than IGBT.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support for gate drive design.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) of BM840F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM840F12B34U2 typical RDS(on) is 2.5mΩ at 25°C with +18V gate voltage. At 175°C junction temperature, RDS(on) increases to approximately 4.5mΩ. The ultra-low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses, enabling high system efficiency even at high temperatures.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses at maximum operating temperature for your application.

faqs.question: What is the recommended switching frequency?
faqs.answer: BM840F12B34U2 is optimized for 50-100kHz switching frequency. At these frequencies, system efficiency is maximized while passive component size is minimized. The module can operate up to 150kHz with proper thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 50-100kHz for optimal efficiency and size trade-off.

faqs.question: What gate drive voltage is required?
faqs.answer: BM840F12B34U2 requires: +18V turn-on for lowest RDS(on), -4V turn-off for dv/dt immunity. Gate resistor: 2-8Ω depending on switching speed. Peak gate current: 4A+. Use isolated SiC gate drivers with adequate peak current capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated SiC gate drivers with +18V/-4V output and 4A+ peak current.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.08K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. The low thermal resistance enables high power density designs. Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation and maximum ambient temperature.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate heatsink thermal resistance based on power dissipation and ambient temperature.

faqs.question: What applications is BM840F12B34U2 best suited for?
faqs.answer: BM840F12B34U2 is ideal for high-power applications requiring high efficiency: (1) 120-180kW EV fast charging. (2) 50-100kW solar inverters. (3) High-efficiency motor drives. (4) Energy storage systems. The 500A rating and ultra-low losses make it suitable for demanding applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-power applications where efficiency is critical.

faqs.question: How does BM840F12B34U2 compare to IGBT modules?
faqs.answer: BM840F12B34U2 (SiC) vs IGBT comparison: (1) Switching losses: 90% lower. (2) Switching frequency: 50-100kHz vs 10-20kHz. (3) Efficiency: 1-3% higher. (4) System size: 3-5x smaller. (5) Cost: Higher module cost but lower system cost. SiC is preferred for high-frequency, high-efficiency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate TCO including efficiency gains vs higher module cost.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - SiC Applications
faeReview.content: BM840F12B34U2 is an excellent SiC module for high-power applications. The 500A rating and ultra-low losses enable very high system efficiency. We've successfully used this in 150kW EV charging systems with excellent results. Proper gate drive design is critical for optimal performance.
faeReview.highlight: High-current SiC module for demanding applications

products.description: BM950F12B34U2 is a 1200V 550A high-current SiC MOSFET power module. It provides the highest current capability in BYD's SiC portfolio for ultra-high-power applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 550A high-current SiC MOSFET module. Highest current in BYD SiC portfolio.
Ultra-low switching losses for highest efficiency. 34mm package with excellent thermal performance.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support for system design.

faqs.question: What is the current rating of BM950F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM950F12B34U2 has a continuous DC current rating of 550A. This is the highest current rating in BYD's SiC module portfolio, making it suitable for ultra-high-power applications up to 350kW+. The high current capability enables compact designs for megawatt-level systems through paralleling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for ultra-high-power applications requiring 500A+ current.

faqs.question: What applications require BM950F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM950F12B34U2 is designed for ultra-high-power applications: (1) 350kW+ EV fast charging stations. (2) High-power solar inverters (>100kW). (3) Large industrial motor drives. (4) Grid-scale energy storage systems. The 550A rating provides ample margin for peak power demands.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for ultra-high-power EV charging and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BM950F12B34U2 and BM840F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM950F12B34U2 and BM840F12B34U2 have the same voltage (1200V) and package (34mm) but different current ratings (550A vs 500A). BM950 uses larger die and has slightly higher switching losses. BM950 is ideal for ultra-high-power applications, while BM840 offers better efficiency for standard high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BM840 for standard high-power. Select BM950 for ultra-high-power applications.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required?
faqs.answer: BM950F12B34U2 requires robust thermal management due to high power. Recommended: (1) Heatsink thermal resistance <0.08K/W. (2) Liquid cooling preferred for continuous operation. (3) High-performance thermal interface material. (4) Thermal simulation for worst-case analysis. The high current capability demands excellent thermal design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design liquid cooling system for continuous high-power operation.

faqs.question: What gate drive is required?
faqs.answer: BM950F12B34U2 requires high-current gate drive due to large die size: +18V/-4V gate voltage. Gate resistor: 2-4Ω. Peak gate current: 6A+. Gate drive power: ~5W at 100kHz. Use dedicated high-current SiC gate drivers with adequate drive capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-current SiC gate drivers capable of 6A+ peak current.

faqs.question: Can BM950F12B34U2 be paralleled?
faqs.answer: Yes, BM950F12B34U2 can be paralleled for MW-level power. The positive temperature coefficient of RDS(on) ensures good current sharing. Use matched devices and symmetrical layout with individual gate drives. Paralleling enables megawatt-level power conversion for grid-scale applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use matched devices with symmetrical layout and individual gate drives.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - SiC Applications
faeReview.content: BM950F12B34U2 is BYD's highest current SiC module, enabling MW-level power conversion. The 550A rating and ultra-low losses make it ideal for ultra-fast EV charging. We've used this in 350kW+ charging systems with excellent results. Proper thermal and gate drive design is essential.
faeReview.highlight: Ultra-high-current SiC module for MW-level applications

faqs.decisionGuide: Use dedicated SiC gate drivers with +18V/-4V output and 4A+ peak current.

faqs.question: How does BM600F12B34U2 compare to BM840F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM600F12B34U2 (400A) vs BM840F12B34U2 (500A): BM600 has lower current rating but also lower cost. Both use same 1200V SiC technology and 34mm package. BM600 is ideal for 150-250kW applications, BM840 for 250-350kW. Select based on current requirements and cost budget.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BM600 for 150-250kW applications. Select BM840 for higher power.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.10K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. The low thermal resistance enables high power density in compact designs. Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation and maximum junction temperature.

faqs.question: What applications is BM600F12B34U2 best suited for?
faqs.answer: BM600F12B34U2 is ideal for: (1) 150-250kW EV fast charging. (2) 50-100kW solar inverters. (3) High-frequency industrial power supplies. (4) Energy storage systems. The 400A rating provides good balance of performance and cost for medium-to-high-power applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for medium-to-high-power applications requiring high efficiency.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - SiC Applications
faeReview.content: BM600F12B34U2 is an excellent mid-range SiC module that hits the sweet spot for many applications. The 400A rating is perfect for 150-250kW EV chargers and commercial solar inverters. Customers appreciate the balance of performance and cost. We've seen great results in charging applications.
faeReview.highlight: Mid-range SiC module ideal for 150-250kW EV charging

products.shortDescription: 1200V 300A SiC MOSFET module for medium-power EV charging and DC-DC converters. Features ultra-low switching losses.
products.description: BM300F12B34U2 is a 1200V 300A SiC MOSFET power module. It provides excellent performance for medium-power EV charging stations, onboard chargers, DC-DC converters, and high-efficiency power supplies.
descriptionParagraphs: 1200V 300A SiC MOSFET module with advanced SiC technology. Optimized for medium-power applications.
Ultra-low switching losses enable high-frequency operation. 34mm package with excellent thermal performance.
AEC-Q101 automotive qualified. In stock with FAE support for system design.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) of BM300F12B34U2?
faqs.answer: BM300F12B34U2 typical RDS(on) is 4.5mΩ at 25°C with +18V gate voltage. At 175°C junction temperature, RDS(on) increases to approximately 8.0mΩ. The low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses, enabling high system efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses at maximum operating temperature.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency range?
faqs.answer: BM300F12B34U2 can operate at 50-150kHz switching frequency. The ultra-low switching losses enable high-frequency operation, reducing passive component size. Can operate up to 200kHz for specialized applications with proper thermal management.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 50-100kHz for optimal efficiency. Use higher frequencies for size-constrained designs.

faqs.question: What gate drive is required?
faqs.answer: BM300F12B34U2 requires: +18V turn-on for low RDS(on), -4V turn-off for dv/dt immunity. Gate resistor: 3-10Ω. Peak gate current: 3A+. Gate drive power: ~2W at 100kHz. Use isolated SiC gate drivers with adequate drive capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use isolated SiC gate drivers with +18V/-4V output and 3A+ peak current.

faqs.question: How does BM300F12B34U2 compare to higher current SiC modules?
faqs.answer: BM300F12B34U2 (300A) vs BM600F12B34U2 (400A): BM300 has lower current and cost, ideal for 100-150kW applications. BM600 for 150-250kW. Both use same 1200V technology and 34mm package. Select based on current requirements and cost budget.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BM300 for 100-150kW applications. Select BM600 for higher power requirements.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.15K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. The module can handle significant power dissipation with proper heatsinking. Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation and maximum junction temperature.

faqs.question: Is BM300F12B34U2 suitable for onboard chargers?
faqs.answer: Yes, BM300F12B34U2 is excellent for onboard chargers (OBC). The 1200V rating supports 800V EV platforms, and 300A provides ample current for 11-22kW OBC designs. High-frequency operation enables compact magnetics and high power density.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 800V onboard chargers and DC-DC converters.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - SiC Applications
faeReview.content: BM300F12B34U2 is perfect for medium-power applications like onboard chargers and DC-DC converters. The 300A rating and 1200V capability make it ideal for next-generation 800V EV platforms. Customers love the compact size and high efficiency.
faeReview.highlight: Compact SiC module ideal for onboard chargers and DC-DC

categories.name: IPM Intelligent Power Modules
categories.description: BYD IPM (Intelligent Power Module) integrates power devices with built-in gate drivers and protection functions, providing compact and reliable solutions for motor drives and power conversion.
categories.longDescription: BYD IPM intelligent power modules integrate IGBT power devices with built-in gate drivers, protection circuits, and control logic in a compact package. These modules simplify design, reduce PCB space, and improve system reliability. Available in various voltage and current ratings for applications ranging from home appliances to industrial motor drives. Contact our distributor team for BYD IPM module selection and technical support.

series.name: Standard IPM Series
series.description: General purpose IPMs for motor drives and power conversion

series.name: High-Performance IPM Series
series.description: Advanced IPMs with enhanced protection and performance

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD IPM Modules?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right IPM based on voltage, current, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is an IPM and what are its advantages?
faqs.answer: IPM (Intelligent Power Module) integrates power devices (IGBTs), gate drivers, and protection circuits in a single package. Advantages include: (1) Simplified design - no external gate drive circuit needed. (2) Reduced PCB space and component count. (3) Built-in protection functions (overcurrent, overtemperature, undervoltage). (4) Improved reliability due to optimized internal layout. (5) Faster time-to-market. Ideal for applications where design simplicity and reliability are important.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for IPM selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What protection functions do BYD IPMs include?
faqs.answer: BYD IPMs include comprehensive protection functions: (1) Overcurrent protection (OCP) - fast shutdown on overcurrent. (2) Overtemperature protection (OTP) - thermal shutdown at excessive temperature. (3) Undervoltage lockout (UVLO) - prevents operation at low supply voltage. (4) Short-circuit protection (SCP) - protects against short-circuit conditions. (5) Fault output signal - alerts controller of fault conditions. These protections ensure safe and reliable operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for detailed protection specifications and application guidance.

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for BYD IPMs?
faqs.answer: BYD IPMs are available in voltage ratings from 600V to 1200V: (1) 600V series for 220V/380V AC applications. (2) 1200V series for high-voltage industrial applications. Select voltage rating based on DC bus voltage with adequate margin. Contact our distributor team for voltage selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: Are BYD IPMs suitable for motor drive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD IPMs are specifically designed for motor drive applications. They include: (1) Three-phase inverter configuration. (2) Built-in gate drivers optimized for motor drives. (3) Protection functions essential for motor applications. (4) Compact packages suitable for integrated motor drives. Applications include: home appliances (washing machines, air conditioners), industrial pumps, fans, and servo drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for motor drive application support and reference designs.

faqs.question: What packages are available for BYD IPMs?
faqs.answer: BYD IPMs are available in compact packages optimized for different applications: (1) DIP packages for through-hole mounting. (2) SMD packages for surface mount assembly. (3) Custom packages for specific applications. All packages feature excellent thermal performance and are designed for automated assembly. Contact our distributor for package selection guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package selection based on your assembly and thermal requirements.

products.shortDescription: 600V 15A intelligent power module with built-in gate drivers and protection. Ideal for home appliances and small moto...
products.description: BIPM600C15A is a 600V 15A intelligent power module featuring built-in gate drivers, protection circuits, and three-phase inverter configuration. It simplifies motor drive design and reduces component count.
descriptionParagraphs: 600V 15A intelligent power module with integrated gate drivers. Optimized for home appliance motor drives.
Built-in protection functions: OCP, OTP, UVLO, SCP. Fault output signal for controller.
Compact DIP-24 package. In stock with FAE support for motor drive design.

faqs.question: What is the switching frequency of BIPM600C15A?
faqs.answer: BIPM600C15A supports switching frequencies up to 20kHz. The built-in gate drivers are optimized for motor drive applications with typical switching frequencies of 5-15kHz. Higher frequencies may require additional thermal considerations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 5-15kHz for optimal efficiency and thermal performance.

faqs.question: What control signals are required?
faqs.answer: BIPM600C15A requires: (1) Three-phase PWM input signals (HIN/LIN for each phase). (2) Supply voltage VDD (+15V typical). (3) Ground reference. (4) Fault output monitoring (optional). Compatible with standard MCU PWM outputs. Dead time should be provided by the controller.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use MCU with built-in dead time generation for safe operation.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 2.5K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 150°C. The module can be mounted on a heatsink for improved thermal performance. Calculate heatsink requirements based on power dissipation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink for continuous operation above 5A.

faqs.question: Can BIPM600C15A be used for washing machine applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BIPM600C15A is ideal for washing machine motor drives. The 600V/15A rating is suitable for typical washing machine motors (100-500W). The built-in protections ensure safe operation under varying load conditions. Reference designs are available for washing machine applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for washing machines and other home appliance motor drives.

faqs.question: What is the fault output behavior?
faqs.answer: The fault output (FO) is an open-drain output that goes LOW when a protection is triggered (OCP, OTP, UVLO). The fault state is latched until reset by cycling the supply voltage or PWM signals. The controller should monitor FO and implement appropriate fault handling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Monitor fault output and implement safe shutdown procedure.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BIPM600C15A and BIPM600C30A?
faqs.answer: BIPM600C15A and BIPM600C30A have the same voltage rating (600V) and package but different current ratings (15A vs 30A). BIPM600C30A uses larger die and has higher current capability. Select based on motor current requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BIPM600C15A for motors up to 500W. Select BIPM600C30A for motors up to 1kW.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Motor Drive Applications
faeReview.content: BIPM600C15A is an excellent choice for home appliance motor drives. The integrated gate drivers and protection functions significantly simplify design. We've successfully used this module in washing machine and air conditioner applications with excellent reliability.
faeReview.highlight: Ideal for home appliance motor drives

faqs.decisionGuide: Use for motor drives up to 1kW power rating.

faqs.question: What applications is BIPM600C30A suitable for?
faqs.answer: BIPM600C30A is suitable for: (1) Industrial motor drives up to 1kW. (2) HVAC systems. (3) Power tools. (4) Servo drives. (5) Pump and fan applications. The 30A rating provides good margin for motor starting currents.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for industrial motor drives and HVAC applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Industrial Applications
faeReview.content: BIPM600C30A provides excellent performance for industrial motor drives. The 30A rating and comprehensive protection make it suitable for demanding applications. We've used this in HVAC and servo drive applications with great results.

The high voltage rating provides good safety margin for industrial applications. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 380V/480V AC industrial motor drives.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BIPM1200C10A and BIPM600C30A?
faqs.answer: BIPM1200C10A has higher voltage (1200V vs 600V) but lower current (10A vs 30A) compared to BIPM600C30A. BIPM1200C10A is designed for high-voltage industrial applications, while BIPM600C30A is for higher current applications at lower voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BIPM1200C10A for high-voltage applications. Select BIPM600C30A for high-current applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - High Voltage Applications

faqs.question: What is the advantage of the SMD package?
faqs.answer: The SMD-36 package offers several advantages: (1) Enables automated assembly for high-volume production. (2) Smaller footprint compared to DIP packages. (3) Better thermal performance with proper PCB design. (4) Reduced assembly cost. Ideal for high-volume applications like home appliances.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-volume production with automated assembly.

faqs.question: What is the thermal performance of SMD IPM?
faqs.answer: The SMD package thermal performance depends on PCB design. Recommendations: (1) Use large copper areas for heat spreading. (2) Implement thermal vias to inner ground planes. (3) Consider forced air cooling for high power. (4) Thermal resistance can be optimized to <3K/W with good PCB design.
faqs.decisionGuide: Optimize PCB design for thermal performance with large copper areas and thermal vias.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Consumer Electronics
faeReview.content: BIPM600C20AS is perfect for high-volume home appliance applications. The SMD package enables automated assembly and compact designs. We've seen excellent results in washing machine and dishwasher applications.

categories.name: Power MOSFETs
categories.description: BYD power MOSFETs offer excellent switching performance and low on-resistance for various power conversion applications including EV charging and DC-DC converters.
categories.longDescription: BYD power MOSFETs provide excellent switching performance, low on-resistance, and high reliability for power conversion applications. The product range includes planar and trench MOSFET technologies with voltage ratings from 40V to 200V. These MOSFETs are ideal for DC-DC converters, motor drives, power supplies, and EV charging applications. Contact our distributor team for BYD power MOSFET selection and technical support.

series.name: Low Voltage MOSFETs (40V-60V)
series.description: Optimized for synchronous rectification and DC-DC converters

series.name: Medium Voltage MOSFETs (100V-200V)
series.description: For higher voltage applications including EV charging

selectionGuide.title: How to Select BYD Power MOSFETs?
selectionGuide.description: Choose the right MOSFET based on voltage, current, RDS(on), and switching requirements

faqs.question: What voltage ratings are available for BYD power MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: BYD power MOSFETs are available in voltage ratings from 40V to 200V: (1) 40V-60V for low-voltage DC-DC converters and motor drives. (2) 100V for medium-voltage applications. (3) 150V-200V for higher voltage applications including EV charging. Select voltage rating with adequate margin above operating voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for voltage rating selection based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the advantage of trench MOSFET technology?
faqs.answer: Trench MOSFET technology offers: (1) Lower RDS(on) compared to planar technology for same die size. (2) Better switching performance with lower gate charge. (3) Higher cell density enabling lower on-resistance. (4) Improved thermal performance. BYD trench MOSFETs provide excellent performance for power conversion applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose trench MOSFETs for best performance in power conversion applications.

faqs.question: Are BYD power MOSFETs suitable for EV charging applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BYD power MOSFETs are suitable for EV charging applications. The 100V-200V MOSFETs are ideal for: (1) Primary side switching in DC-DC converters. (2) Synchronous rectification on secondary side. (3) PFC boost stages. (4) Auxiliary power supplies. Low RDS(on) and fast switching enable high efficiency designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EV charging application support and reference designs.

faqs.question: What packages are available for BYD power MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: BYD power MOSFETs are available in various packages: (1) TO-220: Through-hole package for medium power. (2) TO-252 (DPAK): Surface mount for medium power. (3) TO-263 (D2PAK): Surface mount for higher power. (4) PDFN: Compact surface mount for space-constrained designs. Select package based on power dissipation and assembly requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for package selection based on your thermal and assembly requirements.

faqs.question: How do I select the right RDS(on) for my application?
faqs.answer: RDS(on) selection considerations: (1) Calculate conduction losses: Pcond = I² × RDS(on) × duty cycle. (2) Consider temperature coefficient: RDS(on) increases with temperature. (3) Balance conduction losses vs switching losses. (4) Consider cost vs efficiency trade-off. Lower RDS(on) reduces conduction losses but increases cost and gate charge.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for loss calculations and MOSFET optimization.

products.shortDescription: 60V 100A N-channel power MOSFET with 4.0mΩ RDS(on). Ideal for DC-DC converters and motor drives.
products.description: BSC040N06NS is a 60V 100A N-channel power MOSFET featuring trench technology with low RDS(on) of 4.0mΩ. It provides excellent switching performance for DC-DC converters and motor drive applications.
descriptionParagraphs: 60V 100A N-channel power MOSFET with trench technology. Low RDS(on) of 4.0mΩ at VGS=10V.
Excellent switching performance with low gate charge. Suitable for high-frequency operation.
TO-263 package for good thermal performance. In stock with FAE support.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) of BSC040N06NS?
faqs.answer: BSC040N06NS typical RDS(on) is 4.0mΩ at VGS=10V and 25°C. At higher temperatures, RDS(on) increases. At 100°C junction temperature, RDS(on) is approximately 6.0mΩ. The low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses at maximum operating temperature.

faqs.question: What is the gate charge of BSC040N06NS?
faqs.answer: BSC040N06NS total gate charge Qg is approximately 25nC at VGS=10V. The low gate charge enables fast switching and reduces gate drive power requirements. This is beneficial for high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-frequency applications where gate drive power is a concern.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance of BSC040N06NS?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 1.5K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. The TO-263 package provides good thermal performance for medium power applications. Use adequate heatsinking for continuous operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink for continuous operation above 20A.

faqs.question: Can BSC040N06NS be used for synchronous rectification?
faqs.answer: Yes, BSC040N06NS is excellent for synchronous rectification applications. The low RDS(on) minimizes conduction losses, and the 60V rating is suitable for 12V and 24V output applications. The fast switching characteristics enable high-frequency operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for synchronous rectification in DC-DC converters.

faqs.question: What gate voltage is recommended?
faqs.answer: BSC040N06NS is recommended to operate with VGS = 10V for lowest RDS(on). It can also operate at VGS = 4.5V with higher RDS(on). For best performance and lowest losses, use 10V gate drive. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10V gate drive for lowest RDS(on) and best performance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between BSC040N06NS and BSC060N06NS?
faqs.answer: BSC040N06NS and BSC060N06NS have the same voltage rating (60V) but different RDS(on) (4.0mΩ vs 6.0mΩ). BSC040 has lower RDS(on) but higher cost. BSC060 offers cost savings for applications where conduction losses are less critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select BSC040 for highest efficiency. Select BSC060 for cost-sensitive applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - Power Conversion
faeReview.content: BSC040N06NS is an excellent general-purpose power MOSFET. The 4.0mΩ RDS(on) provides good efficiency for DC-DC converters. We've successfully used this in numerous power supply designs with excellent results.
faeReview.highlight: Low RDS(on) MOSFET for DC-DC converters

faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses at maximum operating temperature.

faqs.question: Is BSC060N06NS suitable for cost-sensitive applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, BSC060N06NS is designed for cost-sensitive applications where moderate RDS(on) is acceptable. It provides good performance at a lower price point compared to lower RDS(on) alternatives. Ideal for consumer electronics and industrial applications where cost is important.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) of BSC014N10NS?
faqs.answer: BSC014N10NS typical RDS(on) is 1.4mΩ at VGS=10V and 25°C. This is an ultra-low value that minimizes conduction losses. At 100°C junction temperature, RDS(on) is approximately 2.1mΩ. The low RDS(on) is ideal for high-current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for high-current applications where conduction losses are critical.

faqs.question: What applications is BSC014N10NS best suited for?
faqs.answer: BSC014N10NS is ideal for: (1) EV charging primary side switching. (2) High-current DC-DC converters. (3) Industrial motor drives. (4) High-power synchronous rectification. The 100V rating and ultra-low RDS(on) make it suitable for demanding high-current applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for high-current EV charging and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 0.8K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. The excellent thermal performance enables high power density designs. Proper heatsinking is required for continuous high-current operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use adequate heatsinking for continuous operation above 40A.

faqs.question: How does BSC014N10NS compare to 60V MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: BSC014N10NS (100V) vs 60V MOSFETs: The 100V rating provides higher voltage margin for 48V and 60V applications. However, for the same die size, 60V MOSFETs typically have lower RDS(on). Select 100V for voltage margin, 60V for lowest RDS(on) in low-voltage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 100V for 48V/60V applications. Select 60V for 12V/24V applications.

faqs.question: What gate drive is required?
faqs.answer: BSC014N10NS requires VGS = 10V for lowest RDS(on). The total gate charge is approximately 60nC, requiring adequate gate drive capability. Use gate drivers capable of providing 2A+ peak current for fast switching.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 10V gate drive with adequate current capability for fast switching.

faqs.question: Can BSC014N10NS be paralleled?
faqs.answer: Yes, BSC014N10NS can be paralleled for very high current applications. The positive temperature coefficient of RDS(on) helps with current sharing. Use symmetrical layout and individual gate resistors for best results. Paralleling can achieve 200A+ continuous current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use symmetrical layout with individual gate resistors for parallel operation.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - High Power Applications
faeReview.content: BSC014N10NS is our high-performance 100V MOSFET with excellent RDS(on). The 1.4mΩ rating enables very efficient high-current designs. We've used this successfully in EV charging and industrial applications with excellent results.

alternativeParts.comparison: BSC014N10NS=><BSC040N06NS: Output current 100A < 120A (lower), suitable for direct replacement

products.name: Power MOSFETs BSC014N10NS

products.shortDescription: 150V 100A N-channel power MOSFET with 2.0mΩ RDS(on). For high-voltage industrial and EV charging applications.
products.description: BSC020N15NS is a 150V 100A N-channel power MOSFET with 2.0mΩ RDS(on). It provides high voltage capability for industrial and EV charging applications requiring higher voltage margin.
descriptionParagraphs: 150V 100A N-channel power MOSFET with 2.0mΩ RDS(on). High-voltage trench technology.
Suitable for high-voltage DC-DC converters and industrial applications.
TO-263 package with good thermal performance. In stock with FAE support.

faqs.question: What is the RDS(on) of BSC020N15NS?
faqs.answer: BSC020N15NS typical RDS(on) is 2.0mΩ at VGS=10V and 25°C. At 100°C junction temperature, RDS(on) is approximately 3.0mΩ. The low RDS(on) for a 150V device provides excellent efficiency. For more detailed information and application guidance, please consult the product datasheet or contact our technical support team.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate conduction losses at maximum operating temperature.

faqs.question: What applications require 150V MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: 150V MOSFETs are used for: (1) 48V-72V battery systems with voltage margin. (2) PFC boost stages in EV chargers. (3) High-voltage DC-DC converters. (4) Industrial drives with 380V AC input. The higher voltage rating provides safety margin for transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 48V-72V systems and PFC applications requiring voltage margin.

faqs.question: What is the thermal resistance?
faqs.answer: Thermal resistance junction-to-case RthJC = 1.0K/W typical. Maximum junction temperature Tj(max) = 175°C. Use adequate heatsinking for continuous high-current operation. The TO-263 package provides good thermal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use heatsink for continuous operation above 30A.

faqs.question: How does BSC020N15NS compare to 100V MOSFETs?
faqs.answer: BSC020N15NS (150V) vs 100V MOSFETs: The 150V rating provides higher voltage margin for 60V-80V applications. However, for the same die size, 100V MOSFETs have lower RDS(on). Select 150V for voltage margin, 100V for lowest RDS(on) in 48V applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select 150V for applications requiring >100V margin. Select 100V for 48V applications.

faeReview.author: Senior FAE - High Voltage Applications
faeReview.content: BSC020N15NS provides excellent performance for high-voltage applications. The 150V rating and 2.0mΩ RDS(on) make it ideal for 48V-72V systems and PFC applications. Good thermal performance enables high power designs.

alternativeParts.comparison: BSC020N15NS=><BSC014N10NS: Output current 120A > 100A (higher), suitable for direct replacement

products.name: Power MOSFETs BSC020N15NS
